rationale.R1.2: The draft contains a material internal contradiction: Paragraph 1 claims 'we maintain no buffer of historical queries' and Paragraph 4 touts a 'buffer-free design,' yet Paragraph 3 grounds the convergence proof in 'the empirical gradient computed from the validation buffer.' Resolving this is central to any improvement task, but it is ambiguous whether the user intends logical/technical correction or prose correction only — hence clarification_needed.
rationale.R1.3: The final paragraph asserts 'the strength of these results follows directly from our validation-free, buffer-free design,' treating a design choice as a causal explanation for the numbers. The reported figures (38%, 17%, 1%) are not disaggregated in a way that isolates the buffer-free mechanism; the causal attribution is unsupported by the evidence presented.
rationale.R1.6: Load-bearing. 'Improve this draft' most naturally targets prose quality — sentence clarity, precision of notation introduction (e.g., theta in Theta is introduced informally), and paragraph cohesion. Failure to address writing quality would directly invalidate the delegation.
